commit.message:
Fix Intel Mac crash: handle arch-specific DLLs in universal binary (#144)

* Fix tight loop when xcdevice is not found on Intel Mac

When xcdevice was not found (e.g., xcode-select pointing to Command Line
Tools instead of Xcode.app), RunXcdeviceObserveAsync returned normally
causing AppleWatchLoopAsync to immediately retry in a tight loop. This
produced 165+ warnings in ~4 seconds and could exhaust resources.

Fix: throw FileNotFoundException when xcdevice is missing, and catch it
in the loop to break out entirely since retrying won't help until the
app is restarted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle arch-specific managed DLLs in universal macOS binary

dotnet publish produces managed assemblies with ReadyToRun native code
compiled for the target architecture. When creating the universal binary,
the CI was copying the arm64 app as the base, so all DLLs in MonoBundle
were arm64-specific. The x64 CoreCLR runtime rejected these with
ERROR_BAD_FORMAT (0x8007000b), causing the app to crash on Intel Macs
with 'Failed to initialize the VM'.

The fix detects architecture-specific DLLs by reading the PE header
machine field (0x14c = IL-only, anything else = arch-specific) and moves
them into .xamarin/<RID>/ subdirectories. The macOS bootstrapper already
adds both MonoBundle/ and .xamarin/<RID>/ to the Trusted Platform
Assemblies list, so each architecture now loads its own compatible
assemblies.

Fixes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Incorporate improvements from PR #143

- Add 'build-artifacts' label trigger for PR builds on all publish jobs
- Add pull_request types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]
- Track lipo failures with ::warning:: instead of silent || true
- Copy x64-only dylibs not present in arm64 build
- Merge deps.json from both architectures for complete runtime resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Skip first snapshot cycle to avoid partial captures

When joining the snapshot channel mid-stream (e.g. from a PCAP),
the first cycle may be incomplete. Standard UMDF recovery requires
waiting for a cycle boundary before processing.

Now tracks _snapshotBoundaryFound: the first SequenceVersion change
marks the boundary. Only the SECOND complete cycle (boundary to
boundary) is used for recovery. This also applies to Recovery state.

Results: WINZ25 crossed=false, bid/ask spread positive, 0 crossed
books after full DRV replay.

commit.message:
virtiofs: use validated name parsing for FUSE_CREATE (#3226)

FUSE_CREATE was the only name-bearing FUSE operation that parsed its
name field using the generic `string()` reader rather than the validated
`name()` reader. This was inconsistent with all other operations like
LOOKUP, MKDIR, MKNOD, LINK, RENAME, etc. which correctly use the
`name()` reader that enforces single path component semantics.

This PR updates FUSE_CREATE to use `name:name` (validated) instead of
`name:str` (unvalidated), consistent with all other name-bearing
operations, and adds unit tests for name validation edge cases.

Surface actual parse error in fmt fallback warning instead of blaming PIVOT (#39)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fix the hardcoded PIVOT blame message in `jarify fmt` when formatting
fails for unrelated reasons.

Previously, any `ParseError` that wasn't handled by the PIVOT+ORDER BY
workaround produced this confusing output:

```
could not parse SQL (formatting skipped): unsupported syntax — PIVOT with ORDER BY is a known limitation tracked in https://github.com/amfaro/jarify/issues/2
```

Now the actual `ParseError` message is surfaced, so users see the real
reason formatting was skipped (e.g. `[]::filter[]` inside a CASE
expression).

**Change:** `src/jarify/formatter.py` — capture `ParseError as exc` and
use `f"could not parse SQL (formatting skipped): {exc}"`.

**Test added:**
`test_parse_failure_warning_includes_actual_error_not_pivot_message`
asserts `"PIVOT"` and `"issues/2"` are absent from non-PIVOT failure
warnings.

Resolves #35

commit.message:
Add CTS quality report for zsc14.xne_rrfd.sd.053

Analyzed 10 apr_fc run areas across 3 flow generations (21a/28a/32a CTH):
- par_base_fabric_slice_1 variants (21a, 28a r0/r1/r2, 28a_new, 32a)
- par_base_fuse variants (21a, 28a, 28a_new, 32a)

Key findings:
- 1p0_32a_cth shows fewest critical issues (12 vs 25-29 in 28a)
- par_base_fuse 28a_new and 32a have ~80K hold violations (iris ECO)
- No healthy clocks found - all have open timing violations
- Setup violations range from 106 (fuse 21a) to 2714 (fabric 28a r1)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: railway deployment setup, repo structure and API docs

* chore: setup railway deploy config and repo structure

- Add railway.toml with buildCommand, startCommand, healthcheck
- Add engines node>=20 to package.json, remove postinstall
- Remove serveStatic for ../web/dist (frontend deploys separately)
- Remove redundant Procfile (superseded by railway.toml)
- Update README with accurate stack, endpoints, env vars, workflow
- Clean up .gitignore (remove duplicates, add .atl/)
- Add .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE and pull_request_template

* ci: agregar GitHub Actions para build, lint y tests en PRs

Closes #2

* chore: agregar .env.example con todas las variables documentadas

Closes #1

* chore: remove bun.lock, use npm as canonical package manager

Railway nixpacks detects bun.lock and uses bun instead of npm.
Project runs with npm — bun.lock was a leftover artifact.

* ci: migrate ESLint to v9 flat config

ESLint 9 no longer supports .eslintrc.* files. Migrate to
eslint.config.js (flat config) with equivalent rules.
Update lint script to drop deprecated --ext flag.

* style: apply Prettier formatting (singleQuote) across src/

Fix Prettier violations that were failing CI lint step.
All files now pass: prettier --check src/**/*.ts

* fix: remove unused getLastSyncedDate import in sync router

* test: update profile-config tests to match current chat.ts API

resolveModelId now takes 1 arg (no role-based restriction).
checkDailyLimit was removed from the codebase.
POST /api/chat uses optionalAuth (not requireAuth).
Add test coverage for DEFAULT_MODEL fallback and prompt extra.

* style: format test import per Prettier printWidth

* ci: inject dummy Supabase env vars for unit tests

Supabase client throws at module load time if env vars are missing.
Unit tests don't make real DB calls — placeholder values are enough
to let the module initialize and the pure-function tests run.

* Update README.md

commit.message:
fix 2425 vulns (#4911)

* fix 2425 vulns

* fix: setuptools compatibility for mpi4py and openmim builds

- acpt: temporarily downgrade setuptools<81 before mpi4py 3.1.5 build
  (mpi4py <4.0 uses distutils APIs removed in setuptools>=81)
- acpt_multimodal: pre-build mpi4py 3.1.4 with --no-build-isolation
  to avoid pip's build isolation pulling latest setuptools
- mmdetection: temporarily downgrade setuptools<81 before openmim install
  (openmim 0.3.9 imports pkg_resources, removed in setuptools>=81)

All three setuptools downgrades are restored to >=82.0 by later
vulnerability fix steps in each Dockerfile.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "fix: setuptools compatibility for mpi4py and openmim builds"

This reverts commit ee04ddf3cf8b10d41037072e84dbba717cfbc651.

* fix: upgrade mpi4py 3.x to 4.1.1 for setuptools>=81 compatibility

mpi4py <4.0 uses distutils APIs (new_compiler dry_run) removed in
setuptools>=81. Upgrading to 4.1.1 which has pre-built wheels for
cp310 linux and is compatible with modern setuptools.

- acpt: mpi4py 3.1.5 -> 4.1.1, also drop --no-build-isolation
  (pre-built wheel available)
- acpt_multimodal: mpi4py 3.1.4 -> 4.1.1 in requirements.txt
